info.title: SuperArchive Developer API
info.description:
Official Developer API for permanent decentralized file uploads powered by Arweave.

### 🔑 Authentication
Include your raw API Key in the `Authorization` header on all requests:
`Authorization: Bearer sa_live_YOUR_API_KEY`

---

### 🔒 File Encryption Strategies
SuperArchive standardizes three distinct encryption options to balance absolute privacy against peak administrative convenience:

1. **Option A: Pre-Encrypted Uploads (Client-Side Encryption)**
   - *How it works*: You encrypt the file locally on your machine (using standard OpenSSL or local AES-GCM libraries) prior to upload, and send it as a standard file. To SuperArchive, this looks like any other regular unencrypted binary file upload.
   - *Pros*: Absolute Zero-Knowledge. Neither our server nor Arweave ever see the plain contents, salt, or keys.
   - *Cons*: You must implement the encryption algorithm locally, manage nonces/IVs, and handle your own keys. **Warning: If you lose your local key, the file is permanently unrecoverable.**

2. **Option B: Zero-Knowledge Server-Side Encryption (`X-Encryption-Key` Header)**
   - *How it works*: You upload a plain file and supply a custom passphrase in the HTTP `X-Encryption-Key` header. Our server performs AES-256-GCM encryption before uploading to Arweave, and immediately discards the passphrase (we never store it in Firestore).
   - *Pros*: On-chain ciphertext privacy without having to write cryptographic logic locally.
   - *Cons*: Decryption requires providing the same `X-Encryption-Key` header during download. **Warning: If you lose your passphrase, the file is permanently lost.**

3. **Option C: Automated Server-Managed Encryption (`enable_server_encryption`)**
   - *How it works*: You upload a plain file with the form parameter `enable_server_encryption=true`. The server automatically generates a random 256-bit AES key/salt, encrypts the file, and securely indexes the key/salt inside our Firestore database.
   - *Pros*: Unmatched convenience. Downloads via our standard API automatically decrypt your file on-the-fly without requiring any header. You can query your key/salt anytime via the `/files/{tx_id}/key` endpoint.
   - *Cons*: Not zero-knowledge to SuperArchive because keys are securely stored in our Firestore (though completely encrypted and zero-knowledge to Arweave L1 and public onlookers).

---

### 📤 File Upload Example (cURL)
```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.sarchive.saltycat.tw/api/v1/SuperArchive" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer sa_live_YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -F "file=@/path/to/your/file.txt"
```

### 📤 Encrypted Upload with Metadata Tags (Python)
```python
import requests

url = "https://api.sarchive.saltycat.tw/api/v1/SuperArchive"
headers = {
    "Authorization": "Bearer sa_live_YOUR_API_KEY",
    "X-Encryption-Key": "your_secure_passphrase"  # Optional zero-knowledge encryption key
}
files = {
    "file": ("document.pdf", open("document.pdf", "rb"), "application/pdf")
}
data = {
    "custom_tags_json": '{"Project": "Alpha", "Category": "Legal"}'  # Optional custom tags
}

response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, files=files, data=data)
print(response.json())
```

post.description:
Core Upload Gateway. Performs split-routing based on file size and daily free quotas.
Exceeding files cost S-points computed atomically via Firestore transactions (1 point per 16MB).

archive_id.description: Permanent archive identifier (identical to the Arweave transaction ID)

sha256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the archived (post-encryption, if applicable) bytes

tx_id.description: Legacy alias of arweave_tx_id, kept for backward compatibility

get.description:
Streams file bytes directly from Arweave gateways. 
Supports decrypting files on-the-fly if 'X-Encryption-Key' header is supplied.
Also supports automated server-managed decryption if 'enable_server_encryption' was utilized on upload.

200.description: Streams the raw (or decrypted) file bytes directly.

get.description:
Lists all files uploaded by the authenticated user, sorted by created_at descending.
Supports pagination via 'limit' and 'offset' query parameters.
Retrieves the remaining S-Points balance from the active developer account ledger.

Returns a comprehensive list of all decentralized permanent files uploaded by this developer 
including complete on-chain metadata, sizes, encryption status, and tags.
Eliminates any need for developers to maintain local database indexes of blockchain transaction IDs.

schema.title: Response List Developer Files Api V1 Developer Files Get

get.description:
Lists all non-deleted API keys belonging to the developer along with active states, 
telemetry, and the cumulative S-Point spending attributed to each specific key.

schema.title: Response List Developer Keys With Spending Api V1 Developer Keys Get

get.description:
Retrieves the server-managed AES key and salt for a specific transaction ID.
Enforces that only the authorized upload owner can fetch their file's encryption keys.

Public, no-authentication endpoint that allows anyone to independently verify
that content archived via SuperArchive is authentic, unchanged, and permanently
retrievable from the Arweave blockchain.

Performs four checks, all of which must pass for `verified` to be `true`:
  1. A database record for this archive_id exists.
  2. The corresponding Arweave transaction data is retrievable from the network.
  3. The SHA-256 hash of the retrieved on-chain bytes matches the hash recorded
     at archive time (content integrity / tamper-evidence).
  4. An archive timestamp exists for the record.

post.description:
Archives a live webpage permanently: downloads an HTML snapshot, extracts
structured AI-ready metadata (title/description/language), computes a
SHA-256 content fingerprint, and uploads the snapshot to Arweave — turning
a URL into a permanently verifiable knowledge asset.

resolved_url.description: Final URL after following any redirects

metadata.title: Example Article Title
metadata.description: A short summary extracted from the page's meta description.

get.description:
Public, no-authentication endpoint that returns the permanent proof information
together with the structured AI-ready metadata recorded for an archive.

This is metadata-only: no summarization, embedding, or LLM processing is performed.
See `/archive/{archive_id}/markdown`, `/summary`, and `/entities` for planned
(not-yet-implemented) AI-derived views of the same content.

Reserved for future use: will return a Markdown-converted rendition of the
archived content (e.g. HTML/PDF -> clean Markdown) for LLM/RAG consumption.
Not implemented yet.

501.description: Not Implemented — reserved for future AI-derived content processing.

example.detail: Markdown conversion is not implemented yet. This endpoint is reserved for future AI-ready content processing.

404.description: The referenced archive_id does not exist.

example.detail: No archive found for the provided archive_id.

get.description:
Reserved for future use: will return an AI-generated summary of the archived
content. Not implemented yet.

501.description: Not Implemented — reserved for future AI-derived content processing.

example.detail: AI summarization is not implemented yet. This endpoint is reserved for future AI-ready content processing.

404.description: The referenced archive_id does not exist.

example.detail: No archive found for the provided archive_id.

get.description:
Reserved for future use: will return AI-extracted named entities (people,
organizations, locations, etc.) from the archived content. Not implemented yet.

501.description: Not Implemented — reserved for future AI-derived content processing.

example.detail: Entity extraction is not implemented yet. This endpoint is reserved for future AI-ready content processing.

404.description: The referenced archive_id does not exist.

example.detail: No archive found for the provided archive_id.

name.title: Name
name.description: Descriptive name of the API key

key_preview.title: Key Preview
key_preview.description: Masked preview of the key (e.g., sa_live_...xxxx)

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 creation date of the key

last_used_at.title: Last Used At
last_used_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the key was last used

s_points_spent.title: S Points Spent
s_points_spent.description: Total S-Points consumed on-chain by this specific key

custom_tags_json.title: Custom Tags Json
custom_tags_json.description: Optional custom metadata tags as a JSON string

enable_server_encryption.title: Enable Server Encryption
enable_server_encryption.description: Whether to apply server-managed AES-256-GCM encryption with keys kept securely in SuperArchive database

title.title: Title
title.description: AI-ready metadata: human-readable title of the archived content

description.title: Description
description.description: AI-ready metadata: short description/summary of the archived content

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: AI-ready metadata: original source URL the content was captured from, if any

language.title: Language
language.description: AI-ready metadata: ISO 639-1 language code of the content, e.g. 'en'

tags_json.title: Tags Json
tags_json.description: AI-ready metadata: JSON array of topical tags, e.g. '["research","AI"]'

archive_id.title: Archive Id
archive_id.description: Permanent archive identifier (currently identical to the Arweave tx_id)

filesize.title: Filesize
filesize.description: Size of the file in bytes

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: MIME content type of the file

sha256.title: Sha256
sha256.description: SHA-256 content fingerprint of the archived bytes, usable for independent verification

custom_tags.title: Custom Tags
custom_tags.description: Custom metadata tags applied to the file on-chain

points_deducted.title: Points Deducted
points_deducted.description: Number of S-Points spent on this upload

is_encrypted.title: Is Encrypted
is_encrypted.description: Whether zero-knowledge AES-256-GCM encryption was applied

is_encrypted_by_us.title: Is Encrypted By Us
is_encrypted_by_us.description: Whether server-managed AES-256-GCM encryption was applied

remaining_s_points.title: Remaining S Points
remaining_s_points.description: Active S-Points balance in the ledger

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL of the webpage to snapshot and permanently archive

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Optional topical tags for AI-ready metadata, e.g. ['research','AI']
